methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $299M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Electra is a Series B-stage climate and energy company founded by Sandeep Nijhawan and Quoc Pham that has raised $299M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of nine private peers operating in the same sector and stage, including Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), and Cemvita ($120M), alongside one public comparable.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a baseline proxy of $299M and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $568M. Peer-median analysis across the five named comparables yields a median valuation of $310M, with the full peer set ranging from $120M to $500M. These methods produced materially different signals, reflecting dispersion in how the broader peer cohort is valued.

The triangulated analysis yields a median estimate of $439M, with a low bound of $264M and a high bound of $653M. The 389-point spread between low and high reflects genuine uncertainty around stage-comparable multiples and peer-relative positioning. Key sensitivities include whether Electra's capital efficiency and market traction warrant positioning above or below peer medians, and whether the stage-progression multiplier of 1.9x appropriately reflects current fundraising conditions.
